name: Hxx
title: HL7 v2 Hxx Open-Ended Segment-or-Group Placeholder
description: Open-ended placeholder for any segment or segment group, per V2.9.1 §1.12 errata. Each occurrence is either a single segment OR a recursive nested group of further Hxx slots. MSH and other transmission-control segments (BHS, BTS, FHS, FTS, DSC) are not permitted. Used in message structure positions where the standard allows an open-ended choice (CH05 query patterns, CH08 MFN_Znn site-defined slot, CH12 ORDER_DETAIL choice, etc.). See ADR-0007 for design rationale and Section 9 of the V2 Management review report for source-clause analysis.

element.definition: Per V2.9.1 §1.12 errata, this slot may contain any segment or segment group, except MSH and other transmission-control segments (BHS, BTS, FHS, FTS, DSC). Each occurrence is either a single segment or a recursive nested group; the slot may repeat to express ordered mixed sequences of segments and groups.

constraint.human: Each Hxx occurrence must contain exactly one of: a single segment (Hxx.segment) or a nested group (Hxx.group).
MSH and transmission-control segments (BHS, BTS, FHS, FTS, DSC) are not permitted in an Hxx placeholder slot (V2.9.1 §1.12).
constraint.expression: segment.empty() or not(segment.type.first().code.endsWith('/MSH') or segment.type.first().code.endsWith('/BHS') or segment.type.first().code.endsWith('/BTS') or segment.type.first().code.endsWith('/FHS') or segment.type.first().code.endsWith('/FTS') or segment.type.first().code.endsWith('/DSC'))

element.short: A single segment instance occupying this Hxx slot
element.definition: When this Hxx occurrence is a single segment (not a nested group), the segment is provided here. Type is the abstract Segment base; any concrete V2 segment may appear, except MSH and the transmission-control segments listed in the slot-level invariant.

element.short: A nested group of Hxx slots (recursive)
element.definition: When this Hxx occurrence is a nested group rather than a single segment, the group's contents are themselves a sequence of Hxx slots, each in turn either a segment or a further nested group. The contentReference enables arbitrary nesting depth.
